programmes.show_name: The Secret World of Richard Nixon
programmes.show_description: This episode reveals Nixon's dangerous and unstable private personality and how it deformed his political career and presidency. Ep1
Born into a middle-class Jewish family, Henia Bryer lost her father, brother and sister during the German occupation - she survived and tells the story of being a Holocaust survivor in her own words.
Revealing life for guests and staff in a typical three-star British holiday hotel. S1 E5
A re-telling of the events leading up to the death of the world's most wanted man. Includes details of the bold military operation that found, hunted and killed Osama Bin Laden.
An in-depth analysis, featuring dramatic historical reenactments, of what happened inside Hitler's bunker during the last hours of the Third Reich.
16 veteran Second World War military vehicles - and a bunch of eccentric collectors - drive 6,000km from Turkey to the beaches of Normandy for an inspiring real-time journey through history.
Examining the history of war tactics. Water covers almost two-thirds of the Earth's surface; we explore assaults from the sea. S1 E4
It was Hitler's deadliest secret - his scientists had promised him an atomic bomb and Nazi domination was assured if he got it first.
August 1945: World War II - the cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in Japan being destroyed by the first atomic bombs used in warfare changes the world forever.
The most gruelling and harrowing of all battles fought in the Second World War, chiefly for the bombing by the Allies of the historic Benedictine Monastery on the summit of Monte Cassino.
By 1938, Hitler was on the road to war. His senior generals devise a plan that would have him arrested and likely killed. Ep 6
As Hitler's ambitions spread beyond Germany, the world drew toward another great war; this one would be fought on many battlefields and for the hearts and minds of the people. E3: Why We Fight
Billy returns home as a qualified doctor, and Jack has a visit from an old Army pal who is in trouble. S2 E3: A Pillowful of Buttercups
Ray quickly steps into his shifty uncle's shoes when Arthur allows himself to be persuaded into retirement.
Ross and Demelza face financial ruin, George plans revenge on Jud, and Francis tries to rebuild his life and extends an invitation to Ross and Demelza. Starring Aidan Turner. S2 E3
As CID investigates a murder linked to a crime syndicate, Sam thinks he is finally coming to grips with the world of 1973, until he meets his 29-year-old father. Starring John Simm. S1 E8
An unexpected visit coincides with Andy and Fergie's Royal Wedding. Alf Garnett has rivals for his affections. In Sickness and In Health S2 E6

A cheerful TV host hides a cruel side. His fame rests on his brother Paul's writing. When a man links the host to their sister's death, Paul suddenly dies.

programmes.show_name: The Secret World of Richard Nixon
programmes.show_description: This episode reveals Nixon's conspiracy to sabotage the 1968 peace talks with Vietnam in order to extend the war and allow a Nixon election victory later that same year. Ep2
A look at the massive part that aircraft played in the Vietnam War with combat footage filmed over North and South Vietnam and the part the war played in shaping many of the weapons used today.
One of the hotel's showers appears inexplicably occupied baffling staff and guests alike, and an Evangelical Christian couple check in. S1 E6
Explore the events which led to the Second World War - from Germany's defeat in 1918 to Hitler's rise to power - and chart the quick successes of Nazi forces up to the surrender of France in 1940. E1
The Battle of Stalingrad was the bloodiest and most destructive battle of the Second World War, taking countless lives and obliterating an ideologically and industrially important Soviet city. E1
We explore the way in which British and American aircraft designers were able to close the gap with the technologically more advanced German and Japanese aircraft of the Second World War.
Counterstrikes can take many forms, and history is full of examples where a numerically inferior army has turned the tide of battle by a brilliant execution of a counterstrike. S1 E5
The Resistance asked for help in late 1943 as some of their top operatives were being held in Amiens prison and needed rescuing from facing interrogation, torture and execution. Ep2: Prison Busters
...You? An airplane shot down over Kigali in Rwanda in 1994 was a single act of terrorism that ignited ethnic tensions between the Hutus and the Tutsis and triggered 100 days of killing.

Six men, including ex-England rugby pro James Forrester, re-enact a mission to discover the true feat of Operation Jaywick, the attack by 14 British and Australian commandos on Japanese ships in WWII.
During 1938, Hitler annexed Austria and the German speaking areas of Czechoslovakia into the Third Reich.
Experience the chaos and turmoil of World War One on a journey through the trenches of France where American soldiers fought alongside their European allies to achieve victory.
Billy cannot get work due to his socialist views, and Tom befriends a Russian Jew hoping to travel to Palestine and looking for Jack who he says can help him. S2 E4: Roubles for the Promised Land
With typical aplomb, Arthur announces the launch of his Daley Into Space business, to flog some satellite dishes.
Needing money to open a mine free of George's influence, Ross strikes a deal with a local smuggler. Starring Aidan Turner. S2 E4
Sam encounters young Tony Crane, a villain he put away in 2006. He realises he is faced with the chance every copper dreams about - stopping the killer before he kills. Starring John Simm. S2 E1
